task: TERMINAL separability experiment, Part 1 -- ACQUIRE higher-resolution flow data to test whether Test B's GEOMETRIC_ONLY finding is a property of the semiannual residency panel or of the phenomenon. DATA ACQUISITION ONLY. No separability/identification claim, no operator run, no co-movement computed -- that is Part 2.
scope_limit: Wrote ONLY this file (build/audit/sep_data_acquired.json) plus raw fetched data under build/data/. Did NOT touch build/model, build/results, build/contracts, or any other build/audit file. DP2/DP3/DP6 NOT started. Makes NO separability or identification claim.

operator_cells_targeted.F3_destination_direction: US holdings OF CYM/HKG/VGB long-term securities (US claims; the reallocation/destination cells US->CYM, US->HKG, US->VGB).
operator_cells_targeted.F4_run_direction: Foreign holdings of US long-term securities by holder country (the USA-column cells across CYM/HKG/VGB and the 12-holder block).
operator_cells_targeted.what_part2_needs_from_higher_resolution: Monthly frequency to expose lead/lag (temporal discriminator) and the substitution SIGN that the semiannual CPIS residency panel could not -- Test B found within-holder US-vs-offshore co-movement +0.4664 (WRONG sign for F3), footprint cosine 0.94, 2022 episode simultaneous across 11/12 holders.

part_a_monthly_tic_slt.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury (TIC system), current SLT tables served from ticdata.treasury.gov resource-center Documents path.

urls_checked.evidence: Home page text: monthly securities data are 'Monthly Holdings of Securities (foreign holdings of U.S. securities, and U.S. holdings of foreign securities)'; annual-survey line 1 'U.S. holdings of foreign securities (U.S. claims; on the usual residence-basis)'; line 1.a same 'but on a nationality-basis'. Confirms (i) BOTH directions exist monthly and (ii) monthly/line-1 is RESIDENCE-basis; nationality-basis is ONLY annual-survey line 1.a.
Table 1: U.S. Long-Term Securities Held by Foreign Residents (F4 direction). Tab-delimited, columns country|country_code|date|holdings|net|valchg by instrument.
Table 2: Foreign Long-Term Securities Held by U.S. Residents (F3 direction = US holdings OF foreign countries' securities).

FRB note confirms monthly cross-border holdings/transactions/valuation 1985-present; footnote that 'Cayman Islands' holdings of U.S. Treasuries are likely mismeasured in the CSLT and SLT' (financial-center mismeasurement, implies residence-based reporting). Did NOT explicitly state residency vs nationality wording (the TIC home page does).

series_files_persisted: build/data/treasury_tic/current/slt_tables/slt_table1.txt (F4: foreign holdings of US LT securities by country)
build/data/treasury_tic/current/slt_tables/slt_table2.txt (F3: US holdings of foreign LT securities by country)
build/data/treasury_tic/current/slt_haven_extract.json (clean CYM/HKG/VGB extract, both directions, 76 monthly rows each)

build/data/treasury_tic/current/slt1d.txt, slt2d.txt (the ARCHIVED/STALE Jan-2023 Publish/ files -- retained to document that the legacy Publish path is stale; the CURRENT data is the resource-center slt_tableN.txt files above)

frequency.evidence: date column is YYYY-MM with consecutive months; 76 distinct month labels per country.

coverage.evidence: From slt_table1.txt and slt_table2.txt: distinct dates 2020-01 ... 2026-04; 2022 (Russia-sanctions episode) fully present (sample rows below include 2022-02 and 2022-03).

granularity.country_resolution: Individual counterparty COUNTRIES, NOT aggregated into 'Caribbean Banking Centers' or 'Other'. CYM/HKG/VGB each appear as their own row with stable country_code.
granularity.china_also_present: China, Mainland, country_code 41408 (direct China cell available, but residency-basis -- see basis caveat).
granularity.instruments: Holdings split by Total / U.S. Treasuries / U.S. Agency Bonds / U.S. Corp. & Other Bonds / U.S. Corp. Equity (Table 1); Total / Foreign Gov't Bonds / Foreign Corp. Bonds / Foreign Corp. Stocks (Table 2); each with Holdings, Net U.S. Sales/Purchases, and Valuation Change.

resolves_F3_destination_cells.direction: US holdings OF CYM/HKG/VGB LT securities (Table 2, Foreign Long-Term Securities Held by U.S. Residents).

resolves_F4_run_cells.direction: Foreign holdings of US LT securities by holder country -- the USA-column cells (Table 1, U.S. Long-Term Securities Held by Foreign Residents).

part_a_monthly_tic_slt.basis_caveat_CRITICAL: TIC SLT is RESIDENCY-based: counterparty = country of residence of the foreign issuer (Table 2) or foreign holder (Table 1), NOT nationality. Grounded from the TIC home page ('U.S. claims; on the usual residence-basis'; nationality-basis is ONLY annual-survey line 1.a) and the FRB CSLT note's Cayman-mismeasurement footnote. THEREFORE: monthly TIC SLT resolves the offshore-HAVEN destination cells (US<->CYM/HKG/VGB) at monthly frequency -- which lets Part 2 test the substitution SIGN and lead/lag at residency granularity that the semiannual CPIS could not -- but it does NOT resolve the China-NATIONALITY reallocation fraction f (US holdings of CYM/HKG/VGB reattributed to CHN nationality). Monthly SLT can test sign/lead-lag at residency granularity; it CANNOT pin f. NOT overclaimed as nationality.
part_a_monthly_tic_slt.what_it_adds_over_semiannual_cpis: Monthly frequency (76 months vs the semiannual CPIS panel) and both directions resolved at the haven level, spanning the 2022 episode -- i.e. the temporal resolution to look for lead/lag and recompute the residency-level co-movement sign that Test B found at +0.4664 on semiannual data. (Whether higher resolution MOVES that finding is the Part-2 question; not adjudicated here.)
part_a_monthly_tic_slt.confidence_reason: Both directions, monthly frequency, 2020-01..2026-04 coverage incl. 2022, and individual CYM/HKG/VGB country codes were read DIRECTLY from the current publisher data files (slt_table1.txt / slt_table2.txt, HTTP 200, fetched 2026-06-29) -- not from memory and not from the stale Jan-2023 Publish/ files. Residency basis grounded from the publisher's own home-page wording.
frictions_noted: The legacy ticdata.treasury.gov/Publish/slt1d.txt and slt2d.txt are STALE (Jan-2023 vintage, single-direction Table 1D / 2D format) -- they are NOT the current data. The current SLT lives at the resource-center Documents path as slt_table1..6 (html+txt). An ingestion pipeline pointed at the old Publish/ path would silently get Jan-2023 data.

Custodial / financial-center bias (carried from the TIC contract; FRB CSLT note repeats it for Cayman): haven holdings are custodial-attributed, so CYM/HKG/VGB residency cells over-weight intermediation relative to beneficial owners. This is a residency-basis property, not a defect of acquisition.
Monthly SLT holdings are partly estimated / benchmarked to annual SHL/SHC surveys (carried from contract).

part_b_post2020_gcap_nationality_restate.publisher: Global Capital Allocation Project (globalcapitalallocation.com) / NBER -- Coppola-Maggiori-Neiman-Schreger (Restated Bilateral External Portfolios, Restatement Matrices); Clayton-Coppola-Dos Santos-Maggiori-Schreger / Florez-Orrego-Maggiori et al. (China in Tax Havens).

urls_checked.status: HTTP 200 (Squarespace JS shell; dataset download links recovered via WebFetch render)
urls_checked.evidence: Lists datasets: Restated Bilateral External Portfolios (.dta/.xlsx/README), Restatement Matrices (.dta/.tar.gz/README), China in Tax Havens (China_Tax_Havens_Data.xlsx + README), 'Time Series For Chinese Firms' Presence in Tax Havens'. No version/year metadata rendered in body text; data-years read from the data files themselves (below).

Sheet1, 398332 data rows, columns: Methodology|Year|Investor|Investor_Name|Asset_Class_Code|Asset_Class|Issuer|Issuer_Name|Position_Residency|Restatement_TH_Only|Restatement_Full|Restatement_Ex_Domestic|Restatement_Sales|Restatement_Sales_Ex. FULL-FILE year scan over all 398332 rows: years = {2007,2008,...,2019,2020}. MAX YEAR = 2020. This IS the counterpart x nationality restatement matrix (Investor x Issuer x Restatement_* by year); it provides the cells but ONLY through 2020.

Sheets Summary/Figure_1a..1d. Figure_1a (CYM/BMU/VGB/Other) -> max year 2020; Figure_1b -> max 2019; Figure_1c & 1d (CYM/BMU/VGB/HKG/CHN/Other) -> max year 2020. Header label 'Residency'. These are China-ISSUANCE shares/levels by haven, NOT US-holdings-of-haven reattributed-to-CHN position cells. Max year 2020.

'Global Capital Allocation' (Florez-Orrego, Maggiori, Schreger, Sun, Tinda), Aug 2023 -- a survey; abstract discusses tax havens/OFCs intermediating global capital but the page did not enumerate post-2020 restated bilateral position cells. Underlying restated data is the GCAP series above (terminates 2020). No post-2020 nationality-restated US-holdings cell recovered.

gives_counterpart_x_nationality_cells.evidence: Restated_Bilateral_External_Portfolios.xlsx provides exactly Investor x Issuer x Asset_Class x Year with Position_Residency and Restatement_* columns -- i.e. US holdings of haven-issued securities reattributed (Restatement_Full / Restatement_TH_Only). But the full-file year scan returns MAX 2020. There is no row dated 2021 or later in any GCAP file fetched today.

coverage.reason: Every GCAP nationality-restatement / China-haven file fetched from the publisher today terminates at data-year 2020 (one sub-figure at 2019). The 2022 Russia-sanctions episode and any post-2020 vintage are NOT covered at the nationality-restated cell granularity.

resolves_F3_destination_cells_nationalit.note: The F3 nationality-restated destination cell (US holdings of CYM/HKG/VGB reattributed to CHN nationality) exists ONLY through 2020. No post-2020 vintage at this granularity. This re-confirms Test A's vintage_test.json freshly, against the publisher's own current data files (not merely citing Test A): the RBEP and China-Tax-Havens files read on 2026-06-29 both stop at 2020.

resolves_F4_run_cells_nationality.note: Same: no post-2020 nationality-basis USA-column cell from GCAP.

part_b_post2020_gcap_nationality_restate.confidence_reason: Max data-year 2020 was read DIRECTLY from the publisher's current data files fetched today (full-file scan of all 398332 RBEP rows; all China-Tax-Havens sheets), not inferred from Test A or memory. The determination NONE_AT_GRANULARITY post-2020 is grounded, not assumed: the files were actually fetched and parsed, and they actually stop at 2020.
part_b_post2020_gcap_nationality_restate.honest_directional_note: This records UNAVAILABILITY of the post-2020 nationality CELL. It does NOT manufacture unavailability -- the files were fetched and parsed. (The coarser-granularity directional signals catalogued in vintage_test.json -- Chinese FDI into Cayman, US-listed Chinese firm counts -- are not re-fetched here; this acquisition task targets the cell granularity, which is absent post-2020.)

unverified: Residency-vs-nationality basis of monthly SLT: grounded from the TIC HOME-PAGE wording ('on the usual residence-basis') and the FRB CSLT footnote; the FRB CSLT note did NOT itself state the residency/nationality distinction explicitly (it described 'liability country'/'holder country' without defining basis). The home-page statement is treated as the authoritative publisher source.

CSLT (Continuous Securities Long-Term) dataset at ticdata.treasury.gov/.../Documents/cslt.zip (announced 05-21-2026) was NOT downloaded/parsed -- it is an alternative unified monthly source (1985-present) that may also carry the same haven cells; its internal structure and basis are UNVERIFIED here. The slt_table1/2.txt files were sufficient to ground Part (a), so cslt.zip was not pulled.

GCAP Restatement Matrices (.dta/.tar.gz) and the 'Time Series For Chinese Firms' Presence in Tax Havens' README content were NOT separately parsed; the Restated_Bilateral_External_Portfolios.xlsx (the cell-level restatement matrix) and China_Tax_Havens_Data.xlsx were parsed directly and both terminate at 2020, which settles the post-2020 question.

NBER w31599 full-text PDF was not parsed for an embedded post-2020 figure; the page-level fetch plus the underlying GCAP data files (max 2020) were used. No post-2020 cell was found, but the full PDF was not exhaustively read.

summary.part_a: OBTAINED -- monthly TIC SLT (current resource-center slt_table1.txt = F4 foreign-holdings-of-US, slt_table2.txt = F3 US-holdings-of-foreign), MONTHLY, 2020-01..2026-04 (2022 covered), CYM/HKG/VGB each individual counterparties. Resolves BOTH operator directions at RESIDENCY granularity, monthly. Does NOT resolve China-nationality f.
summary.part_b: NONE_AT_GRANULARITY post-2020 -- GCAP Restated Bilateral External Portfolios (398332 rows) and China-in-Tax-Havens both terminate at data-year 2020 (one sub-figure 2019), read directly from publisher files on 2026-06-29. No post-2020 nationality-restated counterpart cell exists.
summary.net: Higher-resolution flow data EXISTS for the residency-level haven cells (monthly, both directions, 2022 covered) but NOT for the nationality-restated cells (frozen at 2020). Whether the residency-monthly data moves Test B's GEOMETRIC_ONLY finding is the Part-2 question and is NOT adjudicated here.
